questions.text: What is the clear opening width? (the space the gate needs to cover when fully open)
questions.hint: Measure from post to post, or tell us the driveway width. Typical residential: 10-16 ft, commercial: 20-40 ft.

questions.text: What height do you need for the gate?
questions.hint: Residential typical: 5-6 ft. Commercial/security: 6-8 ft. Measured from ground to top of frame.

questions.text: What material for the gate frame?
questions.hint: 2" square tube 11ga is the industry standard for cantilever gates up to 20 ft.

questions.text: What gauge/thickness for the frame?
questions.hint: 11 gauge is standard for residential/light commercial. Go heavier for gates over 20 ft or high-wind areas.

questions.text: What size tubing for the frame?
questions.hint: 2"x2" handles most residential. 2"x4" or larger for commercial/heavy gates.

questions.text: What type of infill/panel design?
What picket style?
questions.hint: 3/4" square bar or 5/8" round bar are standard. Decorative tops add $2-4 per picket.

questions.text: What spacing between pickets?
questions.hint: 4" max clear space is required by most residential codes to prevent child entrapment.

questions.text: What spacing between flat bars?
What size flat bar?
questions.hint: 1" x 1/4" is the most common for gate infill.

questions.text: What gauge expanded metal?
questions.hint: 13 gauge with 3/4" diamond is standard for security gates.

questions.text: Do you have a photo or sketch of the design you want?
questions.hint: Upload a reference photo or describe the design. We'll work from your vision.

questions.text: How much space is available behind the gate for the counterbalance tail? (cantilever gates need ~50% extra length behind the opening)
questions.hint: A 12 ft opening needs about 18 ft total track length (6 ft tail). If you don't have this space, a swing gate may be better.

questions.text: How many posts? (Cantilever gates typically use 3: 2 for the carriage assembly, 1 catch post at opening end)
questions.hint: 3 posts is standard. The 2 rear posts hold the roller carriages, the front post catches the gate when closed.

questions.text: What size posts?
questions.hint: 4"x4" is minimum for residential cantilever. 6"x6" for commercial or gates over 20 ft.

questions.text: Post installation: new concrete footings needed?
questions.hint: Cantilever posts need deep footings (36-48" below grade, 12-18" diameter). This is critical for gate function.

questions.text: Roller carriage type?
options: Not sure — size based on gate weight
questions.hint: A typical 12 ft residential cantilever gate weighs 400-800 lbs. Always size up from calculated weight.

questions.text: Bottom guide rail type?
questions.hint: Surface mount is cheaper and easier to maintain. Embedded looks cleaner but requires concrete work.

questions.text: Will this gate have an electric operator (motor)?
Which gate operator are you considering?
questions.hint: LiftMaster LA412 is the most common for residential/light commercial. Viking and DoorKing for heavy commercial.

questions.text: What type of latch or lock?
questions.hint: If motorized, an electric strike or magnetic lock is typical. Manual gates usually get a gravity latch or padlock.

questions.text: What finish do you want?
questions.hint: Powder coat is the most common and durable for exterior gates. Galvanizing is best for coastal or harsh environments.

questions.text: What powder coat color?
questions.hint: Black satin is the most popular. Custom RAL colors may add lead time.

questions.text: What paint color?
questions.hint: Describe the color or provide a paint code. We use Rustoleum or similar direct-to-metal paint.

questions.text: Do you need installation?
questions.hint: Full install includes setting posts in concrete, mounting carriages, hanging gate, and adjustments.

questions.text: Where is the job site? (city or zip code for travel time)
questions.hint: We need this to calculate travel time at the on-site rate.

questions.text: Any site access constraints?
questions.hint: These affect install time and may require additional prep.

questions.text: Does this project include adjacent fence sections connected to the gate posts?
options: Yes — fence on one or both sides
questions.hint: Many gate jobs include matching fence sections running from the gate posts to the house or property line.

questions.text: How long is the fence section on the first side? (from gate post to termination point)
questions.hint: Measure from the gate post to where the fence ends (house wall, property corner, etc.). Enter 0 if no fence on this side.

questions.text: How long is the fence section on the second side? (enter 0 if only one side)
questions.hint: Enter 0 if fence is only on one side of the gate.

questions.text: What spacing between fence posts?
questions.hint: 6 ft is standard for residential. 8 ft for commercial or taller fences with heavier material.

questions.text: Should the fence infill match the gate design?
questions.hint: Matching the gate design gives a cohesive look but costs more. Simpler picket infill is common for fence sections.

questions.text: Any decorative elements or custom design details?
questions.hint: Decorative work adds labor and sometimes material. Upload a reference photo if you have one.
